talk_metadata.talk_title: Epidemiology, Etiology, and Clinical Management of Early-Onset Colorectal Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we still don't know the exact etiology for early-onset colorectal cancer"
Speaker noted: "there is no definitive molecular alteration specific to early‑onset patients versus average‑age patients"
Speaker admitted: "we have no data about non‑operative management if you omit radiation therapy"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of molecular stratification for early‑onset colorectal cancer patients

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Dose intensification with FOLFOXIRI in early‑onset rectal cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all early‑onset rectal cancer patients without molecular biomarker selection
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: No ctDNA or molecular signature used for stratification

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Circulating tumor DNA–guided dose intensification with FOLFOXIRI
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Mixed MMR‑stable and MMR‑high patients without separate arms
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Lack of pre‑screening for ctDNA positivity thresholds

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed data that early‑onset colorectal cancer has no unique molecular signature, then concluded that broader molecularly‑guided trials are not feasible

key_data_gaps: No comparison to standard of care

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of molecular biomarkers to select early‑onset colorectal cancer patients for intensified therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "there is no definitive molecular alteration specific to early‑onset patients versus average‑age patients"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omics (DNA, RNA, epigenetic) to discover and validate early‑onset specific biomarkers for trial enrichment

crispro_opportunity.description: Potential to combine ctDNA‑guided dose intensification with novel agents targeting microbiome‑driven pathways
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed interest in microbiome signatures (SBS88, ID18) linked to APC mutations in early‑onset cases
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model microbiome‑tumor interaction networks to identify synergistic drug combinations

unresolved_questions: What environmental or exposomic factors drove the birth‑cohort risk increase after 1990?
Why do early‑onset patients have lower survival in the BreakWater BRAF‑targeted analysis compared to average‑age patients?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Precision Medicine Framework for Pediatric Cancer Survivorship
rhetorical_signals: Speaker noted: "we need to shift from organ‑based surveillance to biology‑driven, risk‑stratified care"
Speaker admitted: "genetic profiling for late‑effect risk is not yet standard of care"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Ignores host genetic modifiers (e.g., CBR3, RARG, SULT4A1) that affect cardiotoxicity risk

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Metformin + resistance training trial for diabetes prevention
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Improving insulin sensitivity in high‑risk pediatric survivors
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad high‑risk definition without genotype‑guided stratification
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: CBR3, RARG, SULT4A1 variants not used for enrollment

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Current survivorship guidelines based on generic organ‑specific screening
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that guidelines are not personalized and lack integration of genomic risk factors
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on reactive care rather than proactive, genotype‑driven monitoring

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed multi‑gene risk models for anthracycline cardiomyopathy, then concluded that standard surveillance remains organ‑based and non‑personalized

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective validation of genotype‑guided surveillance
No long‑term outcome data for epigenetic accelerated aging metric

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to model polygenic cardiotoxicity pathways (CBR3, RARG, SULT4A1) and predict high‑risk survivors
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker described CBR3 GG variant markedly increasing anthracycline toxicity at low doses
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate genotype, dose, and exposure data to generate individualized cardiotoxicity risk scores

crispro_opportunity.description: Implement epigenetic accelerated aging scores to stratify survivors for early interventions
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker introduced epigenetic accelerated aging as a framework linking exposure, genetics, and outcomes
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can compute epigenetic age acceleration from methylation data and link to clinical risk models

unresolved_questions: How can genotype‑guided cardioprotective strategies be operationalized in routine survivorship clinics?
What is the optimal threshold of epigenetic age acceleration to trigger preventive interventions?

assets_to_track: Metformin + exercise trial for survivor diabetes prevention

talk_metadata.talk_title: Institutional Scale Genomic Profiling and Basket Trials for Young‑Onset Cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "DNA sequencing has its limits; we need RNA sequencing to capture drivers"
Speaker admitted: "we have not identified huge differences in somatic mutational profile between young and average onset"
Speaker noted: "basket trials often fail in certain lineages due to mutation type and co‑mutations"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: HER2‑targeted neratinib in HER2‑mutant bladder cancer
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Extracellular domain HER2 mutations in bladder cancer are less sensitive to neratinib

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Neratinib basket trial (HER2 mutations across cancers)
trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: HER2 kinase inhibition across tumor types
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all HER2‑mutant patients without accounting for mutation subtype or co‑mutations
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Mutation domain (kinase vs extracellular) and KRAS co‑mutation status
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Heterogeneous response dilutes overall efficacy signal

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker acknowledged that DNA panel alone misses drivers (e.g., NTRK fusions found only after RNA sequencing)
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on DNA sequencing limits identification of actionable RNA‑level alterations

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that DNA sequencing failed to identify drivers in a melanoma patient, yet continued to emphasize DNA‑based profiling as the primary precision tool

key_data_gaps: Limited RNA‑seq data integration in current clinical workflow
Sparse data on proteomics as a complementary layer

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO‑enabled pipeline that combines DNA, RNA, and emerging proteomics to prioritize patients for basket trials
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker stated: "DNA sequencing is still not telling us everything we need to know"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can fuse multi‑omics to generate composite actionable scores, improving trial enrichment beyond DNA alone

crispro_opportunity.description: Refine basket trial eligibility by incorporating mutation domain and co‑mutation biomarkers (e.g., HER2 kinase vs extracellular, KRAS status)
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker explained why neratinib worked in breast cancer but not in bladder cancer due to mutation type and KRAS co‑mutation
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model lineage‑specific drug‑mutation interactions to suggest biomarker‑driven sub‑cohorts

cited_competitors.name: Larotrectinib
cited_competitors.context: Cited as an example of a tumor‑agnostic FDA approval from a basket trial

cited_competitors.name: Neratinib
cited_competitors.context: Discussed as having variable efficacy across cancer types due to mutation context

unresolved_questions: How will large‑scale RNA sequencing be operationalized for routine clinical decision‑making?
What diagnostic platforms will support future basket trials for ADCs and bispecifics?
